Typography

Type System

ClickMart uses Inter — a clean, highly legible typeface optimized for digital interfaces.

Aa

Inter — Primary Typeface

Free on Google Fonts. Weights used: 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900

↗ Google Fonts
Display & Heading Styles
Display 1
48px · Weight 900 · -3% tracking
Display 2
36px · Weight 800 · -2% tracking
Display 3
28px · Weight 700 · -1.5% tracking
H1 Heading
22px · Weight 700
H2 Heading
18px · Weight 600
H3 Heading
16px · Weight 600
Body & UI Styles
Body 1 — The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
16px · Weight 400 · 1.65 line-height
Body 2 — The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
14px · Weight 400 · 1.6 line-height
Caption 1 — Small descriptive text for images and fields.
14px · Weight 400
Caption 2 — Metadata, timestamps, small labels.
12px · Weight 400
LABEL UPPERCASE
13px · Weight 700 · 8% tracking · UPPERCASE
SMALL UPPERCASE

📸 Photography Guidelines
Hero Images: Use the primary orange (#F97316) for key visuals and CTA overlays at 60% opacity.
Logo Spacing: Maintain clear space equal to the height of the "C" in ClickMart on all sides.
Typography: On brand images, only use Inter. Always align text left or centered in frame.
Style: All brand images should be photorealistic and reflect ClickMart's vibrant, community-first mission.
Usage

Brand Do's & Don'ts

Follow these rules to keep the ClickMart brand consistent and recognizable.

Use the brand orange

Always use #F97316 as the primary action color for CTAs, highlights, and key UI elements.

Change brand colors

Never tint, shade, or substitute the primary orange or blue with colors not in the approved palette.

Use Inter typeface

Use Inter for all digital communications. Weights 400–900 are approved. Free on Google Fonts.

Stretch the logo

Never distort, rotate, apply gradients to, or alter the logo's proportions in any way.

Maintain clear space

Give the logo breathing room equal to the cap-height of the "C" character on all sides.

Place logo on busy images

Use the approved logo variants matched to their background type — never over complex patterns.
